markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Direct-pathway MSN somata; dense terminals in SNr/GPi
markers.notes: Tachykinin peptide marking direct-pathway D1-MSNs (also dorsal horn, hypothalamus).
Adenosine A2A receptor; selective marker of indirect-pathway D2-MSNs.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Purkinje cell somata and dendrites in parasagittal stripes
markers.notes: Defines parasagittal Purkinje cell stripes (Zebrin+/- bands) in cerebellar cortex.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Purkinje cell somata and dendritic arbors
markers.notes: Pan-Purkinje cell marker (also retinal bipolar cells); driver line widely used as Pcp2-Cre.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: CA2 pyramidal somata and proximal dendrites
markers.notes: Gold-standard CA2 pyramidal neuron marker; absent from CA1/CA3.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: CA2 pyramidal somata; also Purkinje cells and granule cells
markers.notes: Calmodulin regulator marking CA2 pyramidal neurons (and cerebellar Purkinje cells).

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: CA1 pyramidal somata across stratum pyramidale
markers.notes: ER membrane protein selectively marking CA1 pyramidal neurons (excludes CA2/CA3).
Definitive DG granule cell TF; persists in adult-born granule cells after DCX is lost.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Peptidergic neuron somata in hypothalamus, LC, amygdala
markers.notes: 29-aa neuropeptide; hypothalamic POA sleep/parental neurons, also locus coeruleus and DRN.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Lateral hypothalamic and zona incerta neuron somata
markers.notes: Lateral hypothalamic peptide; non-overlapping with orexin/Hcrt; sleep and feeding regulation.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Hypothalamic ARC/PVN neurons; also striatum, VTA
markers.notes: Anorexigenic peptide co-expressed with POMC in arcuate nucleus.
Master regulator of GnRH/reproduction; KNDy neurons co-express NKB and dynorphin.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytoplasmic in DAM/CD11c+ microglia and perivascular macrophages; some secreted ECM signal around vessels
markers.notes: Core DAM signature gene marking phagocytic disease-associated microglia and perivascular macrophages in AD/aging.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Lysosomal granular puncta in stage-2 DAM microglia
markers.notes: Stage-2 DAM marker (Keren-Shaul 2017); endolysosomal protease inhibitor strongly upregulated in late-stage disease microglia.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytoplasmic in lipid-handling DAM microglia near plaques
markers.notes: DAM lipid-metabolism marker (Keren-Shaul 2017); links microglial lipid droplet accumulation in aging brain.
Inhibitory immune receptor on DAM/activated microglia; regulates microglial response in neurodegeneration.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytoplasmic in A1-like reactive astrocytes; secreted, some neuropil signal
markers.notes: Liddelow A1 reactive astrocyte marker secreted under neuroinflammation; widely used Abcam/Proteintech IHC validated.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytoplasmic in reactive astrocytes; co-localises with amyloid plaques as ECM-deposited form
markers.notes: Pan-reactive A1 astrocyte marker (Liddelow 2017); accumulates around AD plaques; mouse Serpina3n / human SERPINA3.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytoplasmic and secreted; reactive astrocytes in MS chronic active lesions and AD
markers.notes: Reactive astrocyte glycoprotein and CSF biomarker for MS/AD progression; clock-controlled (Lananna 2020).

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Intermediate filament copolymer with GFAP/vimentin in reactive astrocytes
markers.notes: Type-IV intermediate filament increased in scar-forming reactive astrocytes; partner of GFAP/vimentin.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Endocytic puncta in microglia and oligodendrocytes; AD GWAS risk locus
markers.notes: Second-strongest late-onset AD risk gene; endocytic adaptor with microglial and oligodendroglial expression.
AD GWAS microglial risk receptor; inhibitory Siglec restraining phagocytosis.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytosolic DNA sensor; nuclear and cytoplasmic pools; activated by micronuclei in senescent/aged cells
markers.notes: Cytosolic dsDNA sensor driving cGAS-STING type-I IFN response; key in aging brain and neurodegeneration.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: ER/Golgi reticular pattern on the perinuclear membrane; aggregates after activation
markers.notes: ER-resident adaptor downstream of cGAS; drives type-I IFN signalling in aged/neurodegenerative brain.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytosolic and mitochondrial; neuronal staining lost in ferroptotic regions
markers.notes: Master ferroptosis suppressor; reduced expression marks ferroptotic neuronal death in ALS/PD/AD.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Surface receptor on BBB endothelium and iron-loaded neurons
markers.notes: Iron-uptake receptor; ferroptosis marker and BBB-endothelial label used as a brain-targeting reference.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Ring-shaped lipid droplet coats in glia (lipid-droplet-accumulating microglia, LDAM)
markers.notes: Lipid droplet coat protein labelling LDAM (Marschallinger 2020) and lipid-laden astrocytes in aging brain.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Intermediate filament network in pericytes and vascular smooth muscle
markers.notes: Smooth-muscle/pericyte intermediate filament; complements aSMA for mural cell phenotyping.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Cytotoxic granules in infiltrating CD8 T and NK cells
markers.notes: Cytotoxic granule serine protease in CD8 T / NK infiltrate; marks active killing in MS/aging/AD CNS.

markers.typicalObjectSizeNote: Iron-laden lysosomal granules in microglia at microbleeds; bright autofluorescent puncta
markers.notes: Iron-storage hallmark of microbleed-associated microglia and aging brain; FTH1 IHC complements Prussian Blue.
